div.p: Step 1 — Determine mxFrame Read wal→last_commit_frame mxFrame = last committed WAL frame index
mxFrame = index of the final frame whose db_size > 0 (commit marker). All frames 0..mxFrame are committed.
Step 2 — Determine minReadFrame Scan all active reader snapshots minReadFrame = MIN(reader[i].max_frame) for all readers where is_active == true
If no readers active: minReadFrame = mxFrame (full checkpoint possible)
Compute safe_frame safe_frame = MIN(mxFrame, minReadFrame)
No frames to checkpoint Return 0 (nothing to do)
Initialize loop i = 0 pages_written = 0
Step 3 — Read WAL frame i offset = WAL_HEADER_SIZE + i × WAL_FRAME_SIZE pread(wal_fd, frame_hdr, 24, offset) pread(wal_fd, page_data, 4096, offset + 24) Extract: page_number from frame_hdr[0..3]
Step 4 — Write to database file db_offset = (page_number) × PAGE_SIZE pwrite(db_fd, page_data, 4096, db_offset) pages_written++
Invalidate buffer pool cache If page_number is cached in pool: Remove from page_table Remove from LRU list frame→page_id = INVALID_PAGE_ID
Step 5 — fsync(db_fd) Ensure all written pages are durable on storage
safe_frame == mxFrame ?
ftruncate(wal_fd, 0) Truncate WAL file to zero bytes
Write fresh WAL header • checkpoint_seq++ • Generate new salt1, salt2 • Recompute header checksum • pwrite header (32 bytes at offset 0) • fsync(wal_fd)
Reset in-memory state • wal→frame_count = 0 • wal→last_commit_frame = -1 • wal→write_cksum1 = header.checksum1 • wal→write_cksum2 = header.checksum2 • wal_index_clear(wal→index) • frame_log.count = 0
Step 7 — WAL Reuse Next writer appends from frame 0 in the reset WAL. Sequential append resumes.
Partial Checkpoint Active readers block full reset

Partial Checkpoint State • WAL file NOT truncated • Frames 0..safe_frame are now also in database file (redundant) • Frames safe_frame+1..mxFrame remain ONLY in WAL • WAL index retains entries for frames > safe_frame • New writer appends at frame mxFrame+1

Why partial? Reader A started at max_frame = safe_frame. Frames > safe_frame may contain pages that Reader A needs in their pre-update version (from the database file, not WAL). Truncating WAL would destroy frames that other readers (B, C) with max_frame > safe_frame depend on.
Resolution When all readers with max_frame <= safe_frame call snapshot_end_read(): → Next checkpoint can advance safe_frame → Eventually full reset

WAL state: 5 committed frames (0–4), mxFrame = 4 Active readers: • Reader A: max_frame = 2 (started before txn 3) • Reader B: max_frame = 4 (started after all commits) Checkpoint computes: • minReadFrame = MIN(2, 4) = 2 • safe_frame = MIN(4, 2) = 2 Result: Frames 0, 1, 2 copied to database. Frames 3, 4 remain in WAL only. WAL NOT reset (partial checkpoint). After Reader A ends: • minReadFrame = 4 (only Reader B) • Next checkpoint: safe_frame = 4 • All frames copied → full reset

div.h3: WAL Frame State During Partial Checkpoint
div.p: Frame: [0] [1] [2] [3] [4] Status: ✅ ✅ ✅ 🔒 🔒 ↑ ↑ ↑ copied to Reader A Reader B database boundary boundary file (pinned) ✅ = checkpointed (now in both WAL and DB file) 🔒 = retained in WAL (needed by readers > safe_frame)
